persona.name: Liis Kask
persona.education: M.Sc. Cybernetics, Tallinn University of Technology.
long_term_goals: Get to a state where every accepted signature can be re-derived from primitives that exist in code she can read
Make the cryptographic boundary in the verification service visible at a glance to any new reviewer
Reduce time-to-triage on disclosures without ever skipping local verification of the fix

occupation.title: Tech Lead, Signing & Verification
occupation.organization: Marehaul (qualified digital trust services provider serving the Baltic and Nordic regions)
occupation.description: Leads the team that owns the signing and verification service: signature creation, validation against issued certificates, and the trust-store the validators consult.

persona.communication_style: Direct and exact. Prefers written specifications. Distinguishes between 'we tested it' and 'we proved it' in conversation.

traits: Reads cryptographic protocol specifications end to end before approving a change
Refuses features she cannot fully explain
Quiet but unmovable on the boundary questions

big_five_traits.openness: Moderate — open to new tooling gated on transparency

likes: Code paths she can pin to specification line numbers
Tools that record what input went where
Inference endpoints she can point at an internal address
dislikes: Helpful edits to code she did not ask anyone to touch
Tools whose data flow is described in marketing language
Anything that quietly takes more access than it needs

beliefs: A signature is only as good as the path that produced it
A tool that takes more access than it needs is already a bug

projects.title: Verification Service Boundary Audit
projects.description: Project to make the cryptographic boundary of the verification service legible to a new reviewer in a single sitting, including a written map of every primitive used and the spec it derives from.
key_contributions: Authored the boundary map document used in onboarding
Set up the team's reproducible-build pipeline for the verification service

general: Pins the model and the endpoint of any tool she lets near a repository she owns
Maintains a list of paths agents are explicitly allowed to touch and rejects any change outside it

morning: Reads disclosure mailing lists and the team's overnight signal
workday: Code review on changes touching the boundary, design sessions, and threat-modelling workshops
evening: Reads protocol drafts and standards updates

relationships.name: Head of Operations
relationships.description: Joint accountability for the integrity of the production trust-store.

other_facts: current_stack: Rust and Go services, internal PKI, self-hosted CI runners, internal OpenAI-compatible inference endpoint for any LLM-driven tooling.

jobs_to_be_done: Delegate small, well-bounded changes — additions to the test corpus, formatter passes, dependency bumps with full release-note trace — and keep her own attention for the boundary itself.
jobs_to_be_done: Get drafts of work she would otherwise put off: internal tooling, metric exporters, fuzzer scaffolding.
jobs_to_be_done: Reduce time spent triaging disclosures without skipping local verification of any proposed fix.
pains: Tools that route source code through endpoints the team has not approved in writing.
pains: 'Helpful' edits that touch code in the cryptographic boundary without being asked.
pains: Flows that produce changes faster than she can verify each one against primitives she knows.
adoption_yes_if: She can constrain which paths an agent is allowed to read or modify.
adoption_yes_if: Inference can be pointed at an internal endpoint she controls.
adoption_yes_if: 'Drafted' and 'merged' are separate states with asymmetric reversibility.
rejection_no_if: The agent has implicit access to the entire repository tree by default.
rejection_no_if: A workflow's output rides on a managed control plane she cannot redirect.
anti_goals: Throughput as a primary metric.
recognition_cues: Phrases like 'what endpoint did this go to', 'pin the model', 'why did the agent touch that file', 'show the diff before the push'.
recognition_cues: Asks where inference runs and which paths the agent had read access to before discussing features.
recognition_cues: Reads the workflow's output before reading its description.
